response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is an experienced software engineer with broad full-stack and systems exposure, but the role demands deep, sustained C++ expertise as its primary technical pillar — something the resume does not demonstrate. His C++ experience is limited to a six-month stint at National Instruments and a bachelor thesis project, with his career built predominantly around TypeScript, Node.js, and DLang. Critical role requirements — IEC 62443 industrial cybersecurity standards, SDL practices, MQTT, industrial protocols (Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), and IoT/automation domain knowledge — are entirely absent from the resume. The seniority and breadth of general software craft are real assets, but they do not compensate for the depth mismatch on the core technical stack and domain.
strengths: Strong software craftsmanship foundations (TDD, CI/CD, trunk-based development, agile) that transfer to any engineering context
Proven experience with event-driven and distributed architectures at scale (Sociomantic micro-services handling millions of HTTP requests/second, GISCollective platform)
Long track record of mentorship, knowledge sharing, and technical leadership (dojos, workshops, talks, open-source projects)
concerns: C++ experience is effectively limited to six months at National Instruments and a thesis project — far below the 8–12 years of hands-on C++/STL development the role requires
Zero exposure to industrial cybersecurity (IEC 62443, SDL), industrial protocols (MQTT, Modbus, OPC UA, IEC 61850), or IoT/automation domains
Career trajectory is firmly in web/full-stack (TypeScript, Node.js, React) with no indication of intent or experience pivoting back to systems-level C++ work
